trajectory_summary: The call moved forward and produced a real booking on a warm, self-motivated customer — but Parker's execution was reactive throughout. Jonathan drove the call's direction at nearly every inflection point: he set the comparison frame, volunteered the competitor's system specs, asked what the appointment was for, and requested the phone format. Parker accommodated each pivot without anchoring value, executing button-up, or including the wife. The booking is real but fragile — Jonathan doesn't know what he's walking into, his wife isn't confirmed on the appointment, and the closer has no anchored expectations to build from.
outcome_dispute: The call produced a tentative phone appointment, which is a real outcome on a warm lead. However, the appointment has structural vulnerabilities: wife excluded despite being on title, no button-up elements executed, appointment time ambiguous (transcript shows 'tomorrow at the line' at 09:59 — likely 'one' per Deepgram — but Parker had previously said 'two' at 06:36, and separately referenced 'Friday' at 07:03 when the call's day is unstated). The outcome should be tagged 'conditional_booking' rather than a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Spouse-protocol execution on confirmed co-title holder
primary_coaching_focus.why: Jonathan confirmed his wife is on title at 02:10, she had already expressed design opinions at 01:22, and Parker referenced her at 05:16 — but the appointment was booked without her. This is the single highest-risk structural gap in the call: the closer will almost certainly face a 'I need to talk to my wife' at proposal time, and the setter had three natural entry points to prevent it. Manager should replay 02:10 and drill the pivot: 'Great — since she's on the title and has thoughts on placement, let's make sure she's on the call with us. What time works for both of you?'

key_moments.quote: if I could save money with nothing out of pocket, I kinda feel like it's a no brainer.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jonathan hands Parker the bill anchor and frames the value proposition himself. Parker's response ('Definitely see what we can do to cut that back') captures the data but drops the emotional flip entirely. The schema is explicit: capture alone is necessary but not sufficient — the bill must be flipped into alignment-against-the-utility. Jonathan said 'no brainer' — Parker could have reflected that back and deepened it. Instead the call moved on.

Parker asks the title question correctly and gets confirmation that the wife is on title. The correct next move per schema is to include the wife in the appointment. Parker says 'Okay. Perfect.' and moves on. This is a protocol violation — not an objection-handling failure, but a procedure skip. The wife's aesthetic concern was already flagged at 01:22, making her inclusion even more critical.

key_moments.quote: I actually went through all this. Like I said, with some friends, I have a lot of information for you already.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jonathan signals he's done serious pre-research with people he trusts ('some friends') and has competitive data ready to share. This is a high-qualification signal — he's not a passive lead, he's an active buyer. Parker responds with 'Oh, perfect' and moves to bill collection. The missed move is a competitive-intelligence probe: who are the friends, what did they tell him, what's his current lean? That information would have armed the closer significantly.

key_moments.quote: So it looks like they had 15.39 kilowatt system, two backup batteries. So it's 38 solar panels is what's on our own dish. But if you guys wanna just send over, you know, do what you guys would normally do, I guess, and I'll just compare
key_moments.stewart_read: Jonathan volunteers the competitor's full system spec — 15.39 kW, 38 panels, two backup batteries — and explicitly frames the appointment as a comparison exercise. Parker's response ('we might have different panels') is the only competitive positioning attempted, and it's thin. The schema's already_saw_design rebuttal calls for comparison framing and differentiation on equipment and price points. Parker acknowledged the comparison frame but didn't execute the rebuttal strategy. The closer will face a direct spec-for-spec comparison with no setup.
